auditor: Chartered Professional Accountants (Mike Chang, CPA, CA)
one_time_description: STFN Resorts Inc. consolidated into financial statements after ceasing to qualify as government business enterprise; $1,818,616 in expenses recorded
economic_notes: Nation owns 100% of Skin Tyee Development Ltd. (which owns Tyeewood Forest Resources Inc.), 80% of Natanlii Developments Ltd., 51% of Skin Tyee Horizon North Camp Services Limited Partnership, 14.18% of Chinook Comfor Limited Partnership, and 100% of STFN Resorts Ltd. (consolidated this year). Coastal GasLink Pipeline revenue of $171,324. Active forestry operations generating $685,125.
notes: Audit is a Disclaimer of Opinion due to inability to obtain sufficient appropriate audit evidence for various expenditures. STFN Resorts Ltd. was reclassified from a government business enterprise to a consolidated entity this year, bringing in $6,673,491 in tangible capital assets and significantly impacting net financial assets (swung to net debt of $777,047). Large gifts and donations expense of $576,105. Statement is for fiscal year ended March 31, 2021 but was signed January 30, 2023 — nearly two years late. Total government transfers estimated as ISC $653,289 + BC Gaming $296,636 + FNESC $36,830 + PGNAETA $40,675 + Province of BC $1,213,000 + FNHA $53,209 + Social Housing Fund $2,487 + CMHC-related items; osr calculated accordingly.
